question: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level under standard atmospheric pressure.
The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
The chemical symbol for gold is Au.
Humans have 23 pairs of chromosomes.
Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
Goldfish have a memory of only three seconds.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth measured from sea level.
Bats are blind.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second.
Sharks are mammals.
Sound travels faster in water than in air.
The capital of Australia is Sydney.
The human body contains more bacterial cells than human cells.
Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for his time.
Diamonds are made of compressed coal.
DNA stands for deoxyribonucleic acid.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.
Vikings wore horned helmets in battle.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
A tomato is botanically a fruit.
Humans only use 10 percent of their brain.
Antarctica is the driest continent on Earth.
The currency of Japan is the yuan.
Penguins can fly short distances.
The mitochondrion is often called the powerhouse of the cell.
Albert Einstein won the Nobel Prize for his theory of general relativity.
Honey never spoils if stored properly.
The Amazon River is longer than the Nile.
An octopus has three hearts.
